summary:
The optimal method combines radioactively labeled substances such as water and albumin to track overall fluid movements within tissues effectively. This approach ensures accurate representation of interstitial spaces' dynamic properties rather than relying solely on ion-specific indicators which might not fully capture complex physiological states. Therefore,
\boxed{\text{Combining radioactive water and radiolabled albumin}} offers superior diagnostic utility here due its comprehensive assessment capabilities across multiple components influencing interstitial fluid balance.
